title: Eriostemma ramosii
taxonomy: Eriostemma ramosii is a species of flowering plant in the Arecaceae family. It is a member of the genus Eriostemma, which is a group of palm trees. The plant is native to the Philippines and is often found in tropical forests. Eriostemma ramosii is classified as a monocot, with a single cotyledon in its seeds. This classification is based on its seed structure and other morphological characteristics.

intro: Eriostemma ramosii, also known as the Ramos' Eriostemma, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Asia, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body. The bioactive constituents of Eriostemma ramosii include erioglaucin, eriostemonone, and eriostemonol, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Eriostemma ramosii is used to make herbal preparations, such as infusions and decoctions, to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
